title: China-to-Panama Solar PV Inverter Compliance Gap Matrix
provenance: AI-compiled, multi-model cross-checked, not human-verified. Informational only; confirm with the official regulator and a qualified professional. Not legal/certification/customs/market-access advice.

rows.requirement: EMC and Power Quality — IEC 62920, IEC 61000, and Panama 60 Hz Project Conditions
rows.target_requirement: For Panama PV inverter projects, EMC and power-quality evidence should be prepared in an IEC-based format that project owners, distributors, and conformity reviewers can evaluate. Relevant references include IEC 62920 for PV power-conversion EMC, IEC 61000-series immunity and emission methods, and harmonic / flicker limits applicable to the project size and connection point. Because Panama is a 60 Hz, 120/240 V market, reports generated only under China 50 Hz / 220-380 V conditions should be reviewed for configuration differences before being submitted.
target_standards: IEC 62920 — Photovoltaic power generating systems — EMC requirements and test methods for power conversion equipment
IEC 61000 series — Electromagnetic compatibility emission, immunity, harmonic, and flicker test methods
rows.china_equivalent: Chinese PV inverter EMC evidence commonly appears inside GB/T 37408 or related domestic certification files. Testing may use IEC 61000-family methods, but the scope, report language, grid setup, and acceptance basis are normally China domestic. These reports do not automatically satisfy Panama project submissions, especially where the test configuration does not match the 60 Hz service voltage and firmware profile.
china_standards: GB/T 37408-2019 — 光伏并网逆变器技术要求 (Technical Requirements for Photovoltaic Grid-Connected Inverters) — includes EMC-related requirements
NB/T 32004-2018 — 光伏并网逆变器技术规范 (Technical Specification for Photovoltaic Grid-Connected Inverters)
rows.gap: Gap: Panama submissions should provide IEC 62920 / IEC 61000 evidence that is traceable to the shipped inverter model and its Panama 60 Hz configuration. Exporters should check whether harmonic, flicker, immunity, and emission tests need to be repeated or supplemented for 120/240 V, 60 Hz operation, and whether Spanish summaries or distributor-specific forms are required.

mandatory_status.target: Mandatory in practice where distributor interconnection review, COPANIT / DGNTI conformity review, procurement specifications, or project financing requires EMC and power-quality evidence
mandatory_status.cn: China domestic EMC evidence supports China certification but does not automatically transfer to Panama

rows.verdict: [INFORMATIONAL] Prepare IEC 62920 / IEC 61000 EMC evidence for the exact Panama inverter configuration. Confirm whether China 50 Hz EMC reports need supplementation or retesting for Panama's 120/240 V, 60 Hz grid before distributor or project-owner submission.

rows.requirement: Panama Grid-Connection Route — ASEP, Distributor Review, Anti-Islanding, and 120/240 V 60 Hz Settings
rows.target_requirement: Grid-tied and net-metered solar PV projects in Panama should be treated as project-specific interconnections involving ASEP's electricity-sector framework and the applicable distributor, normally EDEMET, EDECHI, or ENSA depending on service territory. The inverter file should support the Panamanian customer or EPC with model data, certified 60 Hz firmware profile, anti-islanding evidence, protection settings, single-line diagrams, commissioning records, and distributor-facing documentation. Panama uses an Americas-style 120/240 V, 60 Hz low-voltage system, so a China 50 Hz / 220-380 V profile is not an acceptable default.
target_standards: ASEP electricity-sector regulation and project interconnection review
Distributor interconnection requirements for EDEMET, EDECHI, or ENSA service territories
IEC 62116 — Utility-interconnected photovoltaic inverters — Test procedure of islanding prevention measures
IEC 61727 — Photovoltaic systems — Characteristics of the utility interface
Panama project grid profile: 120/240 V, 60 Hz
rows.china_equivalent: Chinese grid-connection evidence commonly references GB/T 19964-2024, GB/T 37408, NB/T 32004, domestic anti-islanding test records, and Chinese utility commissioning documents. These are useful engineering references but are based on China grid practice, normally 50 Hz and 220/380 V. They do not replace ASEP-sector and distributor interconnection review in Panama, nor do they prove that the shipped inverter has been configured and tested for Panama's 60 Hz grid.
china_standards: GB/T 19964-2024 — 光伏发电站接入电力系统技术规定 (Technical Requirements for Connecting Photovoltaic Power Station to Power System)
GB/T 37408-2019 — 光伏并网逆变器技术要求 (Technical Requirements for Photovoltaic Grid-Connected Inverters)
NB/T 32004-2018 — 光伏并网逆变器技术规范 (Technical Specification for Photovoltaic Grid-Connected Inverters)
rows.gap: Gap: Panama requires a locally acceptable interconnection package and a verified 120/240 V, 60 Hz inverter profile. Exporters should prepare IEC 62116 anti-islanding evidence, IEC 61727 utility-interface support, distributor-ready protection settings, and commissioning instructions that lock the Panama profile. Do not ship a China 50 Hz default profile and rely on undocumented field adjustment.

mandatory_status.target: Mandatory for grid-tied and net-metered projects because distributor acceptance and project energisation depend on local interconnection review and correct 60 Hz protection settings
mandatory_status.cn: Chinese grid evidence supports domestic China interconnection and 50 Hz operation but is not transferable to Panama distributor acceptance

rows.verdict: [INFORMATIONAL] Treat Panama grid connection as an ASEP-sector and distributor-specific approval path. Reconfigure and validate the inverter for 120/240 V at 60 Hz, including anti-islanding and reconnection settings, before submitting project documentation. China 50 Hz GB/T evidence is not a substitute for Panama interconnection acceptance.

source.publisher: Autoridad Nacional de los Servicios Públicos (ASEP)

rows.requirement: Distributed Generation and Net-Metering Documentation — ASEP and Panama Distributors
rows.target_requirement: Panama has a strong practical solar context, including distributed generation, net-metering projects, and utility-scale procurement, supported by good solar resource. For inverter exports, the compliance file should be built for the actual project route: customer self-consumption, net-metered distributed generation, commercial rooftop, or utility-scale tender. The file should identify the distributor, service voltage, inverter firmware country profile, anti-islanding method, export-limiting or meter interface if applicable, and commissioning evidence requested by the project engineer or distributor.
target_standards: ASEP electricity-sector framework for regulated public services
ASEP and distributor procedures for distributed generation, self-consumption, and net-metering where applicable
Project-owner technical specifications for utility-scale solar procurement
rows.china_equivalent: China distributed PV approvals and domestic utility commissioning records are project-specific to Chinese grid operators. They may show that the manufacturer can support grid-connected PV projects, but they do not establish Panama net-metering eligibility, distributor acceptance, meter configuration, or ASEP-sector compliance.
china_standards: China NEA distributed PV policy context
rows.gap: Gap: Panama project approval is not a product-only exercise. The supplier should provide Spanish or bilingual technical files, project-specific settings sheets, anti-islanding and power-quality reports, and installation / commissioning instructions suitable for the distributor submission. Where the project uses net metering, confirm metering and export-control expectations before shipment.

mandatory_status.target: Mandatory in practice for grid-tied distributed generation and net-metered projects; exact documentation depends on ASEP rules, distributor procedures, capacity, and service territory
mandatory_status.cn: China distributed PV approvals are domestic project approvals and do not transfer to Panama

rows.verdict: [INFORMATIONAL] Panama's distributed solar opportunity does not remove the interconnection gate. Confirm the distributor, net-metering route, meter interface, and 60 Hz protection profile before shipment. Chinese distributed PV approvals are useful background only and should not be presented as Panama approval.

source.publisher: Autoridad Nacional de los Servicios Públicos (ASEP)

rows.requirement: IEC 62109-1 / IEC 62109-2 Safety Evidence — COPANIT / DGNTI and Project Acceptance
rows.target_requirement: Panama's standards and conformity context is led by COPANIT and DGNTI under MICI. For solar PV inverters, project owners, EPCs, distributors, and conformity reviewers should be expected to ask for internationally recognizable safety evidence, especially IEC 62109-1 and IEC 62109-2 type-test certificates covering the exact inverter model or family. Where COPANIT / DGNTI has adopted or referenced IEC-based standards, the evidence package should map the submitted certificates and reports to those local references.
target_standards: IEC 62109-1 — Safety of power converters for use in photovoltaic power systems — Part 1: General requirements
IEC 62109-2 — Safety of power converters for use in photovoltaic power systems — Part 2: Particular requirements for inverters
COPANIT / DGNTI standards adoption and conformity context
rows.china_equivalent: China's common PV inverter documentation may include GB/T 37408 and voluntary CQC or CGC reports from CNAS-accredited laboratories. These documents may cover safety-related design requirements, but they are China domestic evidence. They should not be presented as automatically equivalent to IEC 62109-1/-2 or Panama COPANIT / DGNTI conformity expectations without a standards mapping and accepted third-party IEC reports.
china_standards: GB/T 37408-2019 — 光伏并网逆变器技术要求 (Technical Requirements for Photovoltaic Grid-Connected Inverters)
rows.gap: Gap: GB/T 37408 safety evidence does not by itself prove IEC 62109-1/-2 compliance for Panama. Exporters should obtain IEC 62109-1/-2 type-test certificates and reports for the specific model, prepare a cross-reference to any COPANIT / DGNTI adopted standard, and confirm whether the project owner or distributor requires an accredited laboratory scope, Spanish summaries, or nameplate / manual changes.

mandatory_status.target: Mandatory in practice where COPANIT / DGNTI conformity review, distributor acceptance, project-owner specifications, or financing requirements call for IEC-based inverter safety evidence
mandatory_status.cn: Voluntary China domestic safety evidence is useful background but not automatic Panama acceptance

rows.verdict: [INFORMATIONAL] Build the Panama safety file around IEC 62109-1/-2 and map it to any COPANIT / DGNTI adopted standard or project specification. A China GB/T 37408 certificate alone should not be treated as Panama safety acceptance.

source.publisher: Dirección General de Normas y Tecnología Industrial (DGNTI / MICI)
